name: Band Bent-Over Rear Lateral Raise
shortDescription: A rear shoulder and upper back exercise using a resistance band in a bent-over position.
instructions: Stand on a resistance band, hinge forward at the hips with a flat back. Grip the band ends and raise your arms laterally to shoulder level, keeping elbows slightly bent, then lower with control.
steps: Stand on a resistance band, feet shoulder-width apart.
Hinge forward at hips, keeping back flat.
Hold band with both hands, arms hanging down.
Raise arms out to sides until parallel with shoulders.
Lower arms back down with control.
breathing: Exhale during the raise, inhale during the lowering.
